page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 112 virtual CPUs across 56 physical cores with simultaneous multithreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The memory configuration consists of 448 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and it comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core capabilities. The server achieves top-tier status in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, PassMark CPU score, and PassMark CPU physics tests. Memory performance is also notable, with cached small-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching top-tier levels at 7,743.53 GB/sec, while uncached large-block read and write bandwidths fall into the strong category.

The hardware profile shows distinct tradeoffs in processing workloads. While database operations and Redis SET operations perform in the strong and top-tier categories respectively, large-scale language model inference presents limitations. Prompt processing for small and medium models is strong, but text generation for a large 70B model is weak, yielding 2.74 tokens per second. This server is suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, database operations, and static web serving.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is an x86_64 server featuring 112 vCPUs and 448 GB of RAM, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 56 physical cores. It does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and Redis SET operations. However, single-core performance and single-threaded compression remain in the average tier. Additionally, while prompt processing for small and medium language models is strong, text generation for large 70B models is weak, producing 2.74 tokens per second. This configuration is suited for highly parallelized workloads, including software compilation, ray tracing, and database management, offering a balanced resource density for multi-threaded tasks without the added cost of bundled accelerators or local storage.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is an x86_64 server with 112 vCPUs and 448 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and strong database capabilities without local storage or GPUs.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-standard-112 is an x86_64 server with 112 vCPUs and 448 GB RAM, featuring top-tier multi-core CPU performance.
tagline: An x86_64 server with 112 vCPUs and 448 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel processing and database workloads.
bullet_points: 112 vCPUs and 448 GB RAM powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth
Strong performance in database operations, software compilation, and ray tracing
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Weak text generation performance for large 70B language models
